steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence is: "The 1958 poem 'The Ghost's Leavetaking' by Sylvia Plath ___ the first use of the word 'dreamscape.'" The subject is "The poem" (singular) and there is no other verb, so the blank must be the main finite verb of the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'having contained': A past participial phrase (non-finite). "The poem having contained" has no main predicate — it is a dangling modifier, not a complete sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'to contain': An infinitive (non-finite). "The poem to contain" is not a complete sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'containing': A present participle (non-finite). "The poem containing" is a fragment with no main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — 'contains': Third-person singular present tense (finite). Agrees with singular subject "The poem" and serves as the main predicate. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
